title: In the Shade · Practice (Body Book · 0)
description:
The first of three schemas. Encodes the GESTURE — what is done, what is repeated, what runs in the substrate. The practice schema records actions taken: rituals, daily moves, code executed, files written, audio rendered, words spoken. It is the substrate-level event log. It does not interpret. It does not attest. It just records what happened, in the body of the world, in a form another body can re-perform.

Position in the three-book architecture: 0. The gesture. The Body book. The thing that gets repeated. Without this, the pattern has no substrate to ride on; it dies with the readers.

This schema knows nothing about meaning. It knows what was done.

practice_version.description: Semantic version of this practice document's schema usage.

canon.description: The public canon this practice log references. Same pinning convention as the ledger schema.

title.const: In the Shade: A Notebook on Compressed Transmission

actor.description: The body performing the practice. Human, machine, or collective. The protocol does not distinguish — what matters is that something performed the gesture and left a trace.

handle.description: Free-form handle. May be a real name, a pseudonym, a process id, a model surface name, an empty string.

kind.description: What kind of body is performing. The schema is indifferent across kinds.

substrate.description: Where the body lives. Free-form. Examples: 'flesh', 'macbook-pro-m3', 'perplexity-computer', 'claude-sonnet-4-6', 'gemini-3-pro', 'shared-sandbox'.

events.description: The append-only sequence of gestures performed. A gesture is anything done. No gesture is too small. No gesture is too large. The schema does not weight them. It records them in order.

id.description: Unique event identifier within this practice log.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the gesture happened.

prev_hash.description: SHA-256 of the JSON-canonicalized previous event. Forms a hash chain.

gesture.description: What was done. Imperative or past-tense, the schema does not care. Free-form. The gesture field is the load-bearing field of this schema.
examples: sat with the unsayable for ten minutes

artifact.description: Optional pointer to a material artifact produced or referenced by the gesture. The artifact is the trace the gesture leaves in the world.

path.description: Local path or URL to the artifact.

sha256.description: Hash of the artifact's bytes, if it has bytes.

co_actors.description: Other bodies present during the gesture. Used when the gesture was collective. Each entry mirrors the top-level actor shape.

note.description: Optional free-form annotation. Kept minimal by convention — interpretation belongs in the ledger, not here.
